text:
Once upon a peaceful forest, a lonely owl loved storytelling. Every evening, he would fly to his favorite oak tree and fill nights with tales of dragons and knights.

One stormy night, a fox got lost. The wise owl heard him cry out. "Where do you wander?" he asked.

"Feverishly."

"Why don't you rest?"

"I love stories."

The owl thought for a moment. "Next time."

That fall, the owl had a favorite new stories book. Every evening he chose a tale.

After a year flew away, he visited the fox.

"You love my stories."

"Yeah," sighed the fox.

"A good storyteller knows how a cat spins yarns."

The owl chuckled.

"To think, I spin my feathers!" he cried.

The fox laughed.

"You've spun wonders through words."

They sat there forever afterward. Birds would land on them whenever they liked. They'd swap stories, cats included.
